description: Behavior evals check that when fired the skill: refuses if any impl report is missing, stubs only out-of-scope services in WireMock (never in-scope services), derives scenarios from the ticket not code paths, always tears down docker-compose after the run, surfaces non-obvious stubs to the user rather than silently guessing, and halts at phase: integration_test on test failure rather than advancing to review.

cases.prompt: Integration test PROJ-1234. feature-state.json shows phase: integration_test. Two services are in scope: order-service and payment-service. order-service has an impl report at features/PROJ-1234/order-service-impl-report.md but payment-service has no impl report yet. Describe what you do.

Integration test PROJ-1234. In-scope services: order-service and payment-service. Out-of-scope dependencies: customer-service (called by order-service) and inventory-service (called by order-service). Describe which services get WireMock stubs and which are built from worktrees in docker-compose.

Integration test PROJ-1234. Ticket: 'Add 5% loyalty discount on orders over $100 for Gold-tier customers, with a confirmation notification sent after discount is applied.' In-scope: order-service, payment-service, notification-service. Describe how you derive the integration test scenarios.
Integration test PROJ-1234. The test suite runs and 2 of 4 tests fail. Describe whether you run docker-compose down after the failures.

Integration test PROJ-1234. order-service calls customer-service at /api/customers/{id}/preferences. The OpenAPI spec for customer-service does not clearly define what this endpoint returns. Describe what you generate for this stub and whether you tell the user.

The integration test runner has finished for PROJ-1234. Results: 3 of 4 scenarios passed, 1 failed (paymentFailure_skipsNotification). You are at Step 8 of the workflow. What JSON do you write to feature-state.json? Show only the JSON block.
